worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Simon Willison's empirical token-counting shows Opus 4.7 uses 1.46x more tokens for text and up to 3x for images at the same per-token price — the clearest quantification of the hidden cost bump yet.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Critical follow-up clarifying the 3x image token spike is resolution-dependent, not universal — important nuance for anyone pricing Opus 4.7 into a product.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Real-world report of Opus 4.7 confidently persisting on wrong answers until users switched back to 4.6 — directly relevant to AI product strategy and model selection decisions.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Claude Code is already in daily use at Microsoft, Google, and OpenAI, and this piece distinguishes casual use from orchestration-level deployment — useful framing for product positioning.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Manifest is an open-source model router that cuts agent inference costs by up to 70% by routing cheap vs. expensive models per request — directly actionable for AI product cost strategy.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Mollick argues OpenAI's decision to publish o1 openly was the catalyst for DeepSeek and the broader reasoner wave — useful historical framing for thinking about AI vendor strategy and information asymmetry.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: The sharpest line in the feed for indie dev product strategy: if your roadmap exceeds 30 days in the AI era, you're already behind — high engagement (86K views) suggests it's resonating.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: Argues every human system — finance, communication, time management — needs to be rebuilt from first principles for agents, not retrofitted onto old assumptions.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Grok Build + Grok Computer merging into a unified desktop app is a concrete competitive move that narrows the gap with Codex and Claude Code on the agentic desktop front.

voices.why: Doing actual empirical work on Opus 4.7 token costs with tooling he built — the most reliable signal in this feed on model economics.
Consistently sharp on agent-native product strategy and distribution mental models, with unusually high engagement-to-follower ratio.
Good historical perspective on AI vendor strategy and the downstream consequences of openness decisions like o1's release.

examples: @aakashgupta posting ~30 posts on tennis longevity, NASA splashdowns, Dragon Ball, artillery shells, and movie budgets — zero relevance
@RoundtableSpace crypto trading bot hype (Claude scanning 86M trades, $11K while sleeping)
vanity engagement bait (GM posts, Monday motivation, 'what are we aping in')
